methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Gladia is a Series A stage company in AI & Software that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Liquid AI ($2.4B), Field AI ($2.0B), and Twelve Labs ($250M), alongside broader public market context including Palantir (PLTR).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $20M total raised as a value proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $40M. Peer-median valuation anchors to the median across the three selected comps at $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M to $2.4B. The peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, reflecting heterogeneity in AI & Software company trajectories and market conditions at Series A.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $34M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.0B. This wide range reflects the core uncertainty: whether Gladia aligns more closely with earlier-stage or later-stage multiples within the AI & Software segment. The stage-progression method implies conservatism relative to peer medians, while peer comparables suggest substantially higher valuations depending on product maturity, revenue traction, and market positioning. Resolution of this disparity hinges on qualitative assessment of Gladia's unit economics, competitive differentiation, and progress toward revenue inflection.
